items.title: German Intelligence Chief: 'We very much hope that the Americans will soon be at our side again'
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Despite Donald Trump's change of course in foreign policy, Bruno Kahl, President of the German Federal Intelligence Service (BND), is counting on continued cooperation with the USA: “At the moment, we can confidently continue our intelligence work for the time being.” At the same time, he appealed for it to be “a joint task of the West” to protect Ukraine from Russia.
“We very much hope that the Americans will soon be at our side again,” said Kahl in an interview with DW in his first public statement since the change in the White House.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Australia's northeast battles floods | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of thousands of people remain without power in Australia after a cyclone brought wild weather to the east coast. Authorities said one man has died and 12 soldiers deployed for assistance were injured after their vehicles crashed in wet conditions.

items.title: Thousands displaced in Israeli raids on West Bank | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Since January, the Israeli army has been carrying out raids on several refugee camps in the occupied West Bank, destroying buildings and infrastructure and displacing tens of thousands of residents. Israel says the camps are a bastion for Palestinian militants and that the raids are for counter-terrorism purposes.

00:00 Thousands displaced in Israeli raids on West Bank
05:12 Mairav Zonszein, Middle East analyst, Crisis Group

items.title: Germany's Green party blocks debt reforms by likely new Government coalition | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany's Green party say they will not support debt reforms proposed by the conservatives and the Social Democrats, potentially sinking their new government before its even been formed.

Conservative leader and likely next chancellor Friedrich Merz said last week that he wants to loosen debt rules to allow higher defense and infrastructure spending. But that would require constitutional changes, making support from Green lawmakers as well as Merz's prospective Social Democratic coalition partners necessary. Green co-leader Felix Banaszak said the party would put forward its own defense proposal.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How much the tariff war could cost US consumers | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>China's tariffs on the US have come into effect today, adding a levy on many US farm products. 

The tariffs of ten to fifteen percent will affect items such as beef, poultry and grains. China is also halting soybean imports from three US companies and stopping purchases of US wood.  

Beijing's measures come after U-S President Donald Trump doubled the universal tariff on Chinese goods from 10 to 20 percent last week. Those are expected to affect 300 Billion dollars worth of goods, and could cost US consumers up to 25 Billion dollars. 

00:00 China's tariffs on the US come into effect
00:42 Kassandra Sundt, DW Business
04:18 Alicia García-Herrero, Economist
08:20 James Chater, DW Correspondent

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Germany: Thousands of flights halted as workers strike at 13 airports | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thousands of flights will be halted across Germany today as workers strike at 13 airports.  

The 24 hour strike includes major hubs Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. It comes a day after hundreds of flights were cancelled at Hamburg airport when ground handlers walked off the job there. The trade union Verdi has called on public service workers, ground staff and aviation security personnel to stop working in disputes over pay. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: UN: 'Entire families' reportedly killed by Syrian militants in Alawite coastal area | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights, a war monitoring group based in the UK, has reported a death toll of over 1,000 people near the coastal town of Latakia in northwest Syria. According to the war monitor, Syria's interim government security forces killed 745 civilians at close shooting range in the Alawite coastal town. In addition, 148 militants loyal to Assad as well as 125 members of the interim government's forces were also killed. The death toll has not yet been independently verified. 
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged Syria, under its current leader Ahmed Al-Sharaa, to hold the "perpetrators" of the killings to account, saying that "radical Islamic terrorists" were responsible for the violence. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Death toll of massacres pushes 1,000 people in coastal area of northwest Syria
2:46 Muzna Dureid, Syrian human rights activist on what Syria's interim government must do to ensure justice for Syria's victims and to hold those behind massacres accountable
#syria #hts #alawite

items.title: Mark Carney chosen to succeed Trudeau as Canada's prime minister and Liberal Party leader | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Sunday, Canada's Liberal Party chose Mark Carney to replace Justin Trudeau as its party leader and Canada's next prime minister. Carney is a former central banker who worked as Director of the Central Bank of Canada until 2013, when he became the first non-British person to become Governor of the Bank of England. Canada will hold federal elections in October 2025, prompted by Trudeau's resignation as prime minister. The Liberals hope Carney can generate increased support for the party after many months of Trudeau's low approval rating. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Mark Carney elected new leader of ruling Liberal Party in Canada
0:33 Sean Mallen, Toronto-based journalist and political commentator, on Mark Carney's career in banking

#markcarney #canada #liberalparty

items.title: Gaza food prices soar after Israel halts deliveries | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has announced that it is cutting off electricity supplies to Gaza — expanding its blockade of the territory. Last week Israel cut off the entry of goods, after the first phase of its ceasefire with Hamas expired. Israel says it is pressuring Hamas to release more hostages before negotiating the second phase of the ceasefire deal, which is supposed to bring a lasting truce.

Chapter Breakdown 
00:00 Israel cuts off Gaza's electricity
3:03 Amande Bazerolle, Doctors Without Borders, in Gaza, on how Israel cutting off Gaza's electricity supply will also impact Gaza's water plants
7:42 Shaina Low, Norwegian Refugee Council, on Israel's siege on Gaza, and how starvation of Palestinians should not be used as a weapon of war

items.title: Russia claims advances in Kursk region ahead of US-Ukraine talks in Saudi Arabia | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russia's Defense Ministry says its troops have recaptured another three villages in the Ukrainian-occupied Kursk region. Former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev said that Ukrainian soldiers in Kursk are almost completely encircled by Russian forces. The developments come days ahead of a planned meeting between US and Ukrainian delegates in Saudi Arabia, aimed at brokering a ceasefire.

Chapters:
00:00 Russia claims advances in Kursk region
00:36 Charles Kupchan, Council on Foreign Relations
